---
title: "About This Template"
description: "Learn how to use and customize this Quarto website template"
---

## About This Template

This is a template for creating **interactive, dynamic websites** using [Quarto](https://quarto.org). It demonstrates how to build web applications that are:

- 🚀 **Fast** — Static site generation with no backend server required
- 🎛️ **Interactive** — Client-side reactivity via Observable JS
- 📊 **Data-driven** — Built-in support for charts, tables, and visualizations
- 🌙 **Themeable** — Light/dark mode toggle included
- 🔍 **Searchable** — Full-text search across all pages
- 📱 **Responsive** — Mobile-friendly layout out of the box

---

## Technology Stack

| Technology | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [Quarto](https://quarto.org) | Document authoring and site generation |
| [Observable JS](https://observablehq.com) | Client-side interactivity and reactivity |
| [Observable Plot](https://observablehq.com/plot/) | Data visualization library |
| [Bootstrap](https://getbootstrap.com) | CSS framework (via Quarto themes) |
| [GitHub Actions](https://github.com/features/actions) | Automated CI/CD pipeline |
| [GitHub Pages](https://pages.github.com) | Static site hosting |

---

## Project Structure

```
.
├── _quarto.yml          # Site configuration
├── index.qmd            # Home page
├── data-viz.qmd         # Data visualization page
├── widgets.qmd          # Interactive widgets page
├── about.qmd            # This page
├── styles.css           # Custom CSS styles
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        └── publish.yml  # GitHub Pages deployment
```

---

## How to Use This Template

### 1. Fork or Clone the Repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/JacobKMcPherson/template_interactive_dynamic_website.git
cd template_interactive_dynamic_website
```

### 2. Install Quarto

Download and install Quarto from [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/).

### 3. Preview Locally

```bash
quarto preview
```

This will start a local development server at `http://localhost:4848`.

### 4. Add Your Content

Create new `.qmd` files and add them to the `navbar` in `_quarto.yml`:

```yaml
website:
  navbar:
    left:
      - href: your-new-page.qmd
        text: Your Page
```

### 5. Build the Site

```bash
quarto render
```

The output will be in the `_site/` directory.

### 6. Deploy to GitHub Pages

Push your changes to the main branch. The GitHub Actions workflow in `.github/workflows/publish.yml` will automatically build and deploy your site to GitHub Pages.

::: {.callout-important}
## First-Time Setup
Before the GitHub Actions workflow can deploy to GitHub Pages, you need to enable GitHub Pages in your repository settings:

1. Go to **Settings → Pages**
2. Under **Source**, select **GitHub Actions**
3. Save the settings
:::

---

## Customization

### Changing the Theme

Edit `_quarto.yml` to change the color theme:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: flatly    # Change to: cosmo, litera, minty, etc.
      dark: darkly     # Change to: cyborg, slate, vapor, etc.
```

See all available themes at [quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-themes](https://quarto.org/docs/output-formats/html-themes.html).

### Adding Interactive Elements

Use Observable JS code blocks (` ```{ojs} `) to add interactive elements:

```{ojs}
//| echo: true
viewof mySlider = Inputs.range([0, 100], { label: "Value", value: 50 })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: true
html`<p>You selected: <strong>${mySlider}</strong></p>`
```

### Navigation Structure

The site uses a top navigation bar. Customize it in `_quarto.yml`:

```yaml
website:
  navbar:
    left:
      - href: index.qmd
        text: Home
    right:
      - icon: github
        href: https://github.com/your-repo
```

---

## License

This template is released under the [MIT License](https://github.com/JacobKMcPherson/template_interactive_dynamic_website/blob/main/LICENSE).
